title: Image Service
description: Browser-side image resizing via canvas: scale images from File or HTMLImageElement sources down to a max dimension while preserving aspect ratio, with configurable output format and quality.

hints.title: Provide the service
hints.content: AXImageService is not providedIn: 'root'. Add providers: [AXImageService] to your component, route, or app.config.ts before injecting it.

hints.title: Browser only
hints.content: Resize uses canvas, Image, and URL.createObjectURL. It runs on the client only — do not call during SSR.

hints.title: Scale-down only
hints.content: maxSize limits the longest side. Images already smaller than maxSize are not enlarged — original dimensions are kept.

hints.title: Not the UI component
hints.content: @acorex/core/image is a processing service. For displaying images in templates use @acorex/components/image (ax-image).

props.name: maxSize
props.description: Maximum width or height in pixels. The longest side is capped at this value; aspect ratio is preserved.

props.name: source
props.description: Input image. File sources are loaded via a temporary object URL.

props.name: quality?
props.description: Encoder quality from 0 to 1. Applies to jpeg/webp. Default: 1.

types.title: AXImageService
types.description: Injectable service for canvas-based image resizing.

content.data:
The @acorex/core/image package exposes AXImageService — a lightweight utility for client-side image compression before upload or storage.

Typical use cases:
• Shrink large photos from a file picker before sending to an API
• Generate thumbnail-sized blobs for avatars or chat attachments
• Convert PNG uploads to JPEG/WebP with reduced quality

The service draws the source image onto an HTML canvas, scales it so the longest side does not exceed maxSize, and exports a Blob via canvas.toBlob().

maxSize — caps the longest dimension (width or height) in pixels:

• Landscape 4000×2000 with maxSize 800 → 800×400
• Portrait 1080×1920 with maxSize 800 → 450×800
• Square 200×200 with maxSize 800 → stays 200×200 (no upscale)

type — output MIME type:

• image/png — lossless, larger files (default)
• image/jpeg — good for photos, supports quality
• image/webp — modern format, supports quality

quality — 0..1 encoder setting for jpeg/webp. Use 0.7–0.9 for uploads to balance size and visual fidelity.

sections.title: Output Format Guide
sections.description: When to use png, jpeg, or webp.

content.data:
Choose the output type based on content and compatibility:

• image/jpeg — best default for photos and avatars; smallest size with quality control
• image/webp — smaller than jpeg at same quality; check browser/API support on server
• image/png — use when transparency is required or for screenshots/diagrams; no quality option (always lossless)

quality only affects jpeg and webp. For png, quality is ignored by the browser encoder.

• @acorex/core/file — AXFileService.choose() to open the file picker programmatically
• @acorex/core/file — AXFileService.blobToBase64() to encode the resized Blob as a data URL
• @acorex/cdk/uploader — AXUploaderService for full upload queue management
• @acorex/components/image — ax-image UI component for rendering images (separate from this service)
